items.title: Core views on AI safety: When, why, what, and how
items.summary: AI progress may lead to transformative AI systems in the next decade, but we do not yet understand how to make such systems safe and aligned with human values. In response, we are pursuing a variety of research directions aimed at better understanding, evaluating, and aligning AI…

items.title: The AI search boom is fueling content budgets before media buys
items.summary: As brands chase visibility in AI search, many are reallocating SEO, content and creator budgets before investing in AI search advertising.

items.title: World’s first Museum of Youth Culture opens in London
items.summary: The newly designed Camden space serves as a permanent home for the various youth movements and trends that have shaped modern Britain

items.title: Using AI to help physicians diagnose rare genetic diseases affecting children
items.summary: Researchers used an OpenAI reasoning model to help diagnose rare diseases, identifying 18 new diagnoses in previously unsolved cases.

items.title: Bristol’s radical spirit informs Bristol Dockyards rebrand
items.summary: How&How recently worked with Brunel’s SS Great Britain in Bristol to reimagine the museum for a modern audience

items.title: How should news organizations label their AI use for audiences? New studies suggest some answers
items.summary: As we’ve reported previously, AI-generated journalism provokes distrust from readers, with audiences favoring a human touch. That might seem obvious, but a lot of questions lie buried in that broad statement. First, with so many ways to use AI, what does “AI-generated” mean, and…

items.title: Creator marketing trends to watch at Cannes Lions 2026
items.summary: Marketers tell us they expect more creators, more content—and potentially more closed-door conversations.

items.title: Substack is launching a sponsorship program
items.summary: On Monday, Substack announced a framework for a native sponsorship program, along with “Creator Kits” to help Substack users build media kits for potential brand partners. (It’s also hired its first head of brand sponsorships, Axios reported.) These tools will make it easier for…

items.title: News sites are the new newspapers: People are abandoning them for social media
items.summary: News sites are rapidly becoming the newspapers of the digital age. And you know what happened to newspapers. Worldwide, people in all age groups are ditching news sites and publishers’ apps in favor of social media and video networks. In fact, 18- to 34-year-olds are abandoning…

items.title: Base44 is one of the most-viewed brands on YouTube. Here’s a look at its ad strategy
items.summary: We chatted with Omer Shai, CMO of parent company Wix, about how the vibe-coding platform is looking to win over users in a crowded category.
